reasoning: The Lean statement captures the informal claim exactly. `[Group G] [Fintype G]` with `hG : card G > 2` encodes "G is a finite group with |G| = n > 2". The subgroup H appears as a free variable `(H : Subgroup G) [Fintype H]`, which is the standard way to express "for all subgroups H" at theorem-statement level; the `Fintype H` instance is automatic for subgroups of a finite group (trivially derivable), so its inclusion doesn't restrict the scope. The conclusion `card H ≠ card G - 1` matches "|H| ≠ n−1"; since card G > 2, natural-number subtraction behaves correctly. No hypotheses or quantifiers are silently added or dropped, and no types are changed.
